p.seg: Robert by the grace of God bishop of St Andrews to all sons of Holy Mother Church Greeting and Blessing:

Let those to come as well as those present know that I have granted in perpetual alms to God and to the church of Holyrood in Edinburgh and to the canons serving God there the church of Tranent with everything that rightfully belongs to it, free and quit from every exaction, as Waleran the chaplain ever had and held it well, reserving the tenure of Waleran the chaplain himself for as long as he remains in secular life, just as King Malcolm has given and affirmed by his charter the aforesaid church of Tranent to the church of the Holy Cross and to the canons serving God there by the advice of his barons. I therefore will and by this my charter make firm that after the departure of Waleran the aforesaid canons shall hold that church as freely and quietly as they have and hold their other alms and possessions, reserving the episcopal right.

With these witnesses, Thorald the archdeacon, Aiulf the dean, Waleran himself being present and suggesting it, Swain of Fishwick, William the bishop’s chaplain, Aelward the chaplain, John the bishop’s nephew, Ralph the bishop’s nephew.
